channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Hacker News] JSON-LD Explained for Personal Websites
item.description: JSON-LD Explained for Personal Websites

item.title: [Hacker News] Burnout is real for open source maintainers
item.description: Burnout is real for open source maintainers

item.title: [Latent Space] [Exclusive] $250 off AI Engineer tix til Monday
item.description: [Exclusive] $250 off AI Engineer tix til Monday

item.title: [Hacker News] Anthropic to Require ID Verification for Certain Capabilities Starting July 8
item.description: Anthropic to Require ID Verification for Certain Capabilities Starting July 8

item.title: [Hacker News] Identity verification on Claude
item.description: Identity verification on Claude

item.title: [Hacker News] The case against geometric algebra (2024)
item.description: The case against geometric algebra (2024)

item.title: [GitHub Trending] ZhuLinsen/daily_stock_analysis — LLM 驱动的多市场股票智能分析系统：多源行情、实时新闻、决策看板与自动推送，支持零成本定时运行。 LLM-powered multi-market stock analysis system with multi-source market data, real-time news, decision dashboard, automated notifications, and cost-free scheduled runs.
item.description: Open-source LLM-powered system analyzes multi-market stock data, integrates real-time news, provides decision dashboards and automated notifications with free execution.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] bytedance/deer-flow — An open-source long-horizon SuperAgent harness that researches, codes, and creates. With the help of sandboxes, memories, tools, skill, subagents and message gateway, it handles different levels of tasks that could take minutes to hours.
item.description: ByteDance releases deer-flow, an open-source SuperAgent harness that orchestrates research, coding, and creation tasks through integrated sandboxes, memories, and subagents.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/mukul975 — 754 structured cybersecurity skills for AI agents · Mapped to 5 frameworks: MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, MITRE ATLAS, D3FEND & NIST AI RMF · agentskills.io standard · Works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex CLI, Cursor, Gemini CLI & 20+ platforms · 26 security domains · Apache 2.0
item.description: Dataset of 754 cybersecurity skills for AI agents mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, MITRE ATLAS, D3FEND, and NIST AI RMF.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/topoteretes — Cognee is the open-source AI memory platform for agents. Give your AI agents persistent long-term memory across sessions with a self-hosted knowledge graph engine.
item.description: Open-source Cognee enables AI agents to maintain persistent memory across sessions using a self-hosted knowledge graph engine.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/asgeirtj — Extracted system prompts from Anthropic - Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Claude Code, Claude Design. OpenAI - ChatGPT 5.5 Thinking, GPT 5.5 Instant, Codex. Google - Gemini 3.5 Flash, 3.1 Pro, Antigravity. xAI - Grok, Cursor, Copilot, VS Code, Perplexity, and more. Updated regularly.
item.description: Collection of extracted system prompts from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and xAI models maintained as an updated GitHub repository.

item.title: [Hacker News] The 100k Whys of AI
item.description: The 100k Whys of AI

item.title: [Hacker News] Building reliable agentic AI systems
item.description: Building reliable agentic AI systems

item.title: [Hacker News] Your brain was never designed for this much bad news
item.description: Your brain was never designed for this much bad news

item.title: [Hacker News] When I reject AI code even if it works
item.description: When I reject AI code even if it works

item.title: [Hacker News] Slow breathing modulates brain function and risk behavior
item.description: Slow breathing modulates brain function and risk behavior

item.title: [Hacker News] Agency stole bestselling author's book, used AI to relaunch as their own
item.description: Agency stole bestselling author's book, used AI to relaunch as their own

item.title: [Hacker News] Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot has died in a plane crash
item.description: Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot has died in a plane crash

item.title: [Hacker News] Loupe – A iOS app that raises awareness about what native apps can see
item.description: Loupe – A iOS app that raises awareness about what native apps can see

item.title: [GitHub Trending] twentyhq/twenty — The open alternative to Salesforce, designed for AI.
item.description: Twenty is an open-source alternative to Salesforce CRM platform with AI integration.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] jamiepine/voicebox — The open-source AI voice studio. Clone, dictate, create.
item.description: jamiepine/voicebox is an open-source AI voice studio enabling voice cloning and voice-based content creation.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] 1jehuang/jcode — Coding Agent Harness
item.description: jcode is a coding agent harness framework trending on GitHub.

item.title: [Hacker News] Temporary Cloudflare accounts for AI agents
item.description: Temporary Cloudflare accounts for AI agents

item.title: [Hacker News] Bootimus – A Self-Contained PXE and HTTP Boot Server
item.description: Bootimus – A Self-Contained PXE and HTTP Boot Server

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: [AINews] not much happened today

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CREDENCE: Claim Reduction for Decomposition & Enhanced Credibility -- Semantic Metrics and Convergence Analysis
item.description: CREDENCE framework replaces Jaccard with semantic similarity metrics for decomposing compound claims into atomic claims, with formal convergence analysis.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Leverage Is Not Reach: A Control-Window Law for Single-Neuron Steering in Language Models
item.description: Researchers develop a control-window framework to predict when single-neuron interventions in language models coherently control behavior without causing output collapse.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] AtomMem: Building Simple and Effective Memory System for LLM Agents via Atomic Facts
item.description: AtomMem achieves state-of-the-art performance by organizing atomic facts into hierarchical structures for long-term memory in LLM agents, overcoming context window limitations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Prompt, Plan, Extract: Zero-Shot Agentic LLMs Workflows for Lung Pathology Extraction from Clinical Narratives
item.description: Zero-shot agentic LLMs achieved 0.893 Micro-F1 extracting lung pathology data, nearly matching supervised approaches without manual training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Large Language Models Do Not Always Need Readable Language
item.description: Large language models can work with compact, unreadable text formats while preserving semantic information, enabling significant context compression.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Almost Intelligent Revolution: Options for Scaling Up Deliberation and Empowering People with AI
item.description: Research examines LLMs for scaling deliberation across language groups while addressing linguistic biases and empowering marginalized communities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] REDACT: A Systematically Controlled Multilingual Benchmark for Personal Information Detection
item.description: REDACT, a multilingual PII detection benchmark spanning 25 languages and 13,427 records, reveals LLM-based detectors are more robust than rule-based systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Light-weight Pronunciation Assessment via Discrete Speech Token Surprisal
item.description: Unsupervised pronunciation assessment using speech token surprisal and alignment features achieves near-supervised performance without requiring labeled learner corpora.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] GEMS: Geometric Constraints Enable Multi-Semantic Superposition in LLMs
item.description: GEMS enables multi-directional activation steering in LLMs using geometric constraints to prevent model collapse from concurrent semantic directions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Source-Grounded Data Generation for Text-to-JSON Learning
item.description: STAGE generates text-to-JSON training data using LLMs validated against spreadsheets, improving structured data extraction from documents.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] IHUBERT: Vector-Based Semantic Deduplication and Domain-Balanced Pretraining for Persian Resources
item.description: IHUBERT, a 125M-parameter Persian language model trained with semantic deduplication, achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple NLU benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Self-Preference Is Weak or Absent in Verifiable Instruction-Following Revision: A Four-Model Test Under Genuine Authorship
item.description: Study finds no evidence of self-preference bias in four LLM families when evaluating corrections to their own text.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] HydraHead: From Head-Level Functional Heterogeneity to Specialized Attention Hybridization
item.description: HydraHead hybridizes full and linear attention at the head level to improve long-context performance while reducing computational complexity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Does Streaming Tool Use Help? Characterizing Tool-Intent Stabilization in Streaming Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: Research characterizes when streaming RAG helps by measuring tool-intent stabilization, finding 73.9% of queries admit substantial latency hiding.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] From Texts to Scores: Tracing the Emergence of Essay Quality Representations in Large Language Models
item.description: LLMs encode essay quality linearly in hidden representations that emerge progressively and correlate with individual neuron activations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MedRLM: Recursive Multimodal Health Intelligence for Long-Context Clinical Reasoning, Sensor-Guided Screening, Evidence-Grounded Decision Support, and Community-to-Tertiary Referral Optimization
item.description: MedRLM framework provides long-context clinical reasoning by recursively coordinating specialized agents for heterogeneous patient data including EHR, imaging, and sensor signals.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ReNikud: Audio-Supervised Hebrew Grapheme-to-Phoneme Conversion
item.description: ReNikud improves Hebrew grapheme-to-phoneme conversion for text-to-speech using weak supervision from unlabeled Hebrew audio.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Pitch Spelling Jazz Lead Sheets, Solo Transcriptions, Classical Piano and Monophonic Scores
item.description: Algorithm estimates pitch spelling and key signatures for musical scores using two-stage optimization on jazz, classical, and monophonic music datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CzechDocs: A Multiway Parallel Dataset of Formatted Documents for Minority Languages in Czechia
item.description: CzechDocs introduces a multiway parallel dataset of formatted documents in Czech and minority languages to evaluate format-preserving machine translation systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
item.description: A shared activation direction enables detection and mitigation of language model misalignment induced by insecure code fine-tuning across architectures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
item.description: New annotation framework reveals AI systems fail on Nigerian discourse from context gaps rather than translation, improving register classification accuracy 40 points.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
item.description: PsyScore integrates neural scoring with psychometrically-aware feedback generation, adapting instructional scaffolding to student proficiency levels.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
item.description: A new multilingual dataset provides expert-curated, fact-grounded counterspeech examples against hate speech and misinformation in multi-turn dialogues across five languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
item.description: LLM alignment using implicit user feedback from eye-tracking and mouse data outperforms text-based reward models, improving accuracy from 55% to 64%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Beyond Global Replanning: Hierarchical Recovery for Cross-Device Agent Systems
item.description: H-RePlan hierarchically separates device-local and cross-device recovery strategies for multi-device agents, improving completion rates and reducing inference costs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
item.description: StylisticBias benchmark shows that roughly 15 visual attributes—primarily age, body type, and fashion—account for 80% of social bias in multimodal AI models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Gender Bias in LLM Hiring Decisions: Evidence from a Japanese Context and Evaluation of Mitigation Strategies
item.description: Study finds pro-female bias in LLM hiring decisions across five models in Japanese context, with candidate names identified as the primary bias source.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] How Linear Is a Transformer Feed-Forward Block? Per-Block Linear Recoverability Is Learned, Not Architectural
item.description: Transformer feed-forward block linearity is learned per-block and highly variable, not determined by architectural design.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Beyond the GUI Paradigm: Do Mobile Agents Need the Phone Screen?
item.description: Mobile coding agents using command-line interfaces outperform GUI-based approaches across multiple benchmarks, showing particular strength in bulk operations and cross-app workflows.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Thermodynamic Signatures of Reasoning: Free-Energy and Spectral-Form-Factor Diagnostics for Hallucination Detection in Large Language Models
item.description: Researchers propose Free-Energy Signatures, a training-free method for detecting LLM hallucinations using thermodynamic analysis of attention mechanisms, outperforming spectral baselines.

item.title: [Hacker News] LLMs Are Complicated Now
item.description: LLMs Are Complicated Now

item.title: [Hacker News] Amazon drops Sam Altman movie after announcing OpenAI partnership
item.description: Amazon drops Sam Altman movie after announcing OpenAI partnership

item.title: [Hacker News] Companies rein in AI usage as costs strain budgets
item.description: Companies rein in AI usage as costs strain budgets

item.title: [Hacker News] Is AI ruining our skills? Early results are in – and they're not good
item.description: Is AI ruining our skills? Early results are in – and they're not good

item.title: [Hacker News] John Jumper to join Anthropic
item.description: John Jumper to join Anthropic
